opp.ais_why:
• Across only 180 chars of NextStep ‚Äî a single 4 Mar 2026 entry ‚Äî the full content reads: 'Met with Proc Mgr and IT team - GJ working on CPO meeting after Raya. To share the demo video and set up the detailed demo F2F meeting in 1st week of Feb.' Zero AI mention. Product scope is eProcurement only (no AI SKUs).
• Procurement Manager and IT team have been met; CPO meeting is being arranged post-Hari Raya; demo video shared and F2F demo planned. BD_Engagement is Unengaged. With no AI products in scope and no AI dialogue captured, the deal is a focused traditional e-procurement evaluation.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is very thin (180 chars, one note). Score 2 reflects single-product e-procurement scope with no AI dimension whatsoever.

sources: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager (Decision Maker contact role): 'save our man hours by about half more than half a day' per vendor registration
Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager: 'Management has green-lit vendor exploration with a rough budget of US$200K/year'
Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026: 11-person procurement team, digital transformation in flight (PEMS, new ERP/HRMS), eProcurement to integrate via API
metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated operational pain with quantified time savings on the 14 Jan 2026 intro call. The procurement manager (named Decision Maker contact role) stated that manual vendor registration from due-diligence reports and Excel consolidation for remote suppliers 'would effectively save our man hours by about half more than half a day' per registration cycle. The buyer is running an 11-person procurement team launching a digital transformation (new Plantation Estate Management System in flight, new ERP/HRMS to be selected), and the eProcurement system is one piece of that transformation, with a rough budget envelope of USD 200k/year mentioned by the buyer on the same call. However, no ROI model, no aggregate annual cost/waste baseline, and no CFO-level quantified business case have been discussed or documented in the 163 days since. The value case remains qualitative (ease of use, vendor experience, compliance, integration) rather than a hard-dollar savings target tied to a compelling event. With zero follow-up since 14 Jan and the rep's 1 Jun note flagging 'poor fit', this value case was never developed beyond the initial statement. The next move is to re-engage the buyer, reconstruct whether the digital transformation timeline and budget are still live, and if so, co-build a quantified savings model (time, headcount, error reduction, compliance lift) anchored to their Q3/Q4 PEMS go-live as the forcing function.

sources: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026: procurement manager (Decision Maker contact role) engaged, articulated pain/criteria, stated 'management said go explore'
163 days of silence since 14 Jan call; no logged reply to Zycus collaterals/demo commitment
Next Step 1 Jun 2026: 'Limited engagement. Poor fit, and consider to drop in next 90 days'

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. The 14 Jan 2026 intro call included the procurement manager (Decision Maker contact role) and a sourcing lead (Noratikah, mentioned in transcript but not a Salesforce contact). The procurement manager was engaged and articulate about pain and criteria, and stated management had green-lit exploration, which are positive signals. However, there is no evidence of advocacy (no internal introductions, no shared documents, no competitive intelligence, no agenda-pushing), and critically, the buyer has been silent for 163 days — no reply to the collaterals/videos promised by Zycus on 14 Jan, no follow-up on the Feb demo commitment, and no engagement after the 4 Mar meeting (which has no recording so content/outcome are unknown). The rep's 1 Jun note flags 'limited engagement, poor fit, consider to drop', which signals the relationship is cooling or dormant, not developing. The 4 Mar note mentions 'GJ working on CPO meeting after Raya', suggesting an attempt to escalate to the Chief Procurement Officer, but no CPO contact has been logged. With zero buyer engagement in 163 days, any prior champion signal has faded to passive or at-risk. The next move is to re-engage the Decision Maker contact (procurement manager) directly, assess whether the digital transformation and vendor selection are still live, and if so, ask for an introduction to the CPO and a finance/EB stakeholder to convert a passive operational contact into an active multi-threaded champion network, or honestly disqualify if the buyer has moved on.

sources: Salesforce Competitors__c: empty
Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026: buyer stated 'checking a box of all service providers', no vendor names

No competitor named in any Next Step, task, or call in 163 days
competition.narrative: No competitor has been named or identified in any Avoma call, Salesforce field, Next Step entry, or task. The Competitors__c field is empty per ground truth. On the 14 Jan 2026 intro call, the buyer mentioned they are using 'ProcureHere' (a shared license system with the relevant stakeholder, license-based, vendors pay per company), which is the incumbent being replaced, but ProcureHere is the current tool, not a competitive alternative in the new vendor selection. The buyer stated they are 'checking a box of all these service providers' and will initiate vendor selection in Q2/Q3 2026, which implies a multi-vendor shortlist, but no other vendor names (e.g. SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Jaggaer, local providers) have been mentioned. The buyer referenced Mybase (a Malaysia-specific procurement platform they helped build in 2012) in a historical context (the procurement manager's prior experience), but did not name it as a current alternative. With 163 days of silence and no progression past Qualified, competitive intelligence is zero. This is a real knowledge gap. The wedge, if the deal were live, would be Zycus's enterprise eProcurement + API integration + AI-driven automation (SSM/Experian vendor registration, agentic sourcing) + local Malaysia presence and references (Maybank, TM, DRB-Hicom, IJN) + ease-of-use for non-tech-savvy vendors, which maps directly to the buyer's stated criteria. But with the deal cold and no known competitors, the competitive position is 'unknown — deal has not reached multi-vendor evaluation'. The next move is to re-engage the buyer, confirm whether vendor selection is still planned, and if so, ask directly who else is being evaluated to understand the shortlist and tailor positioning.

sources: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager: 'save our man hours by about half more than half a day' per vendor registration
Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager: 'suppliers in Sabah region face challenges due to lack of internet connectivity... manual registration... monthly consolidation'
Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager: 'ProcureHere license-based, vendors pay per company, we don't prefer it'
Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager: 'category system needs improvement... I wanna rebuild it altogether, policy, process, SOPs'
Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026: 'eProcurement to integrate via API with PEMS (in flight) and new ERP (to be selected)'
identify_pain.narrative: The buyer articulated specific, confirmed operational and financial pain on the 14 Jan 2026 intro call. Pain owner: the 11-person procurement team led by the procurement manager (Decision Maker contact role) and the sourcing lead. Confirmed pain points: (1) Manual vendor registration — extracting info from due-diligence reports (SSM via Experian) and manually populating eProcurement fields, 'save half more than half a day' per supplier. (2) Remote supplier friction — suppliers in Sabah (Borneo) lack reliable internet, forcing a dual-track (online + manual Excel) vendor registration and monthly consolidation for the approved vendor list, creating compliance risk and manual toil. (3) Vendor cost barrier — current ProcureHere system is license-based and vendors must pay per company to register, reducing vendor participation and buyer's negotiating leverage. (4) Category/sourcing mechanics — current category-code-based tender notification is manual and clunky; buyer wants to rebuild the entire sourcing process (policy, process, SOPs). (5) Lack of integration — current system is standalone; new eProcurement must integrate via API with the in-flight PEMS and future ERP to enable end-to-end procurement-to-pay and eliminate manual handoffs. (6) Spend visibility gap — no consolidated spend reporting; manual Excel aggregation. All six pain points are confirmed, buyer-stated, and tied to the digital transformation initiative (PEMS + ERP + eProcurement). However, the pain has not been quantified beyond the 'half a day' registration time-save, and with 163 days of silence the urgency and pain-owner engagement are unconfirmed as of today. The next move is to re-engage the pain owner (procurement manager), confirm the pain is still acute, and co-build a quantified impact model (hours saved, error reduction, vendor participation lift, compliance improvement) tied to the PEMS/ERP go-live timeline.

sources: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026: Carl Kimball mentioned 'migration... we have local partners in Malaysia... make that real cheap', no further detail
No InfoSec, legal, MSA, SOW, or procurement-channel discussion in any recorded interaction
paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, InfoSec, procurement-channel, or implementation mechanics have been discussed or documented in any interaction. The 14 Jan 2026 intro call focused on functional fit and integration (API with PEMS and future ERP); the buyer mentioned data migration as a future step and Carl Kimball (Zycus APAC head) noted that Zycus uses local Malaysia partners for migration to keep it 'cheap' and 'tactical', but no SOW, MSA, InfoSec questionnaire, legal review process, or implementation timeline has been scoped. The buyer is a large Malaysian GLC (government-linked company) in a regulated sector (plantation/agriculture), so InfoSec, data residency, and procurement compliance are likely material gates, but none have surfaced. There is no evidence of a partner/SI channel (the one contact role marked 'Decision Maker' is on a buyer domain, not a reseller). With 163 days of silence and no progression past Qualified, paper process is entirely unknown. The next move, once buyer engagement is re-established, is to surface the paper path early: ask about InfoSec/legal review requirements, data residency, approval authorities, preferred contracting vehicle (direct vs partner/reseller), and integration/implementation ownership (Zycus, buyer IT, or a local SI), and align these to the PEMS Q4 go-live timeline if still live.

sources: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026: procurement manager (Decision Maker contact role) stated 'management just said go and explore', budget-setting after shortlist
Next Step 1 Jun 2026: '4th Mar - Met with Proc Mgr and IT team - GJ working on CPO meeting after Raya' — CPO meeting planned but not logged as completed
OpportunityContactRole: one Decision Maker (procurement manager, partner domain), zero Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor roles
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged in any recorded interaction. The 14 Jan 2026 intro call included the procurement manager (listed as Decision Maker contact role) and a sourcing lead, both operational/functional roles. The procurement manager stated that 'management just said go and explore' and that budget-setting would come after vendor shortlisting, and mentioned a rough envelope of USD 200k/year, but no CFO, finance executive, or C-level sponsor appeared on the call or has been named in any subsequent activity. Carl Kimball (Zycus APAC head, the deal owner's manager per ground truth) asked directly on the call whether management supported the investment and flagged the enterprise price point; the buyer acknowledged the envelope but did not name who controls the commercial decision or signs off on a 6-figure SaaS spend. The rep's 4 Mar note references 'working on CPO meeting after Raya', suggesting an attempt to reach the Chief Procurement Officer, but no CPO meeting has been logged and no further progress on EB mapping is evident. With 163 days of silence and no EB contact, this is a critical gap. The next move is to re-engage the Decision Maker contact and explicitly request an introduction to the CFO or the executive sponsor (likely the CPO or the GCEO's office, given the digital transformation scope spans PEMS + ERP + eProcurement) to validate budget authority and timing before investing further effort.

sources: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026: 'management said go and explore... checking a box of all service providers... meet criteria, then budget setting'
Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026: 'Q2/Q3 initiate process of selection... PEMS Q4 implementation... new ERP/HRMS not finalized'
Next Step 1 Jun 2026: '4th Mar - Met with Proc Mgr and IT team - GJ working on CPO meeting after Raya'
decision_process.narrative: The 14 Jan 2026 intro call revealed a multi-stage buyer process: (1) management green-lit vendor exploration (pre-budget), (2) vendor shortlisting against criteria, (3) budget-setting and business case after shortlist, (4) vendor selection in Q2/Q3 2026, (5) data migration and integration with PEMS (planned Q4 2026 go-live), and (6) eventual integration with a new ERP/HRMS (not yet selected). The procurement manager stated 'obviously I need to call in Zycus... we are checking a box of all these service providers... whether they can meet our criteria, and then only we are going to talk about the budget setting'. This implies a multi-vendor evaluation (a shortlist, not sole-source) with formal budget approval post-shortlist. The call also referenced a broader digital transformation spanning PEMS, ERP, HRMS, and eProcurement, suggesting cross-functional steering (IT, procurement, finance) and likely C-level (CFO, CPO, or GCEO office) sign-off, though no approval chain has been documented. The rep's 4 Mar note mentions 'Met with the relevant stakeholder and IT team - GJ working on CPO meeting after Raya', indicating an IT stakeholder joined and a CPO escalation was planned, but neither the IT contact nor the CPO meeting has been logged since. With 163 days of silence and no stage progression, the process has stalled at step 1 (exploration). The buyer's original Q2/Q3 selection timeline is now (Jun 2026), but the deal has not moved. The next move is to re-engage the Decision Maker contact, confirm whether the PEMS/ERP/eProcurement roadmap and Q2/Q3 selection window are still live, and if so, request a multi-stakeholder session (procurement, IT, finance/CPO) to present fit against criteria and map the formal approval path.

sources: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager: 'ease of use... vendors may not be tech-savvy... straightforward e-tendering, e-bidding... spend reporting... API integration-friendly with PEMS'
Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026: SSM/Experian API for auto vendor registration, 'save half more than half a day' manual effort
Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026: 'Q2/Q3 initiate process of selection'
decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer stated four explicit evaluation criteria on the 14 Jan 2026 intro call: (1) ease of use for non-tech-savvy vendors (a primary concern given suppliers in remote Sabah with limited connectivity), (2) a workable eTendering and eBidding system (replacing the current shared ProcureHere license model where vendors pay per company), (3) spend reporting and visibility (current system requires manual Excel consolidation of online and offline registrations), and (4) API integration-friendly architecture to connect with the new Plantation Estate Management System (PEMS) under implementation, and eventually a new ERP/HRMS to be selected. The buyer also articulated a specific automation need: pull due-diligence data from the relevant stakeholder (company registry) via a provider like Experian to auto-populate vendor registration fields and generate prequalification reports, cutting manual effort by 'half more than half a day' per supplier. These are confirmed, buyer-stated criteria. However, no formal RFP, no weighted scorecard, no technical/security/integration requirements document, and no structured use-case validation or POC have occurred in the 163 days since the call. The buyer mentioned a Q2/Q3 vendor selection timeline on the call (that window is now, Jun 2026), but with zero activity the criteria have not been tested, expanded, or prioritized. The decision process remains at discovery/qualification with no progression to formal evaluation. The next move is to confirm whether the digital transformation (PEMS Q4 go-live, ERP selection) is still on track, and if so, propose a structured demo and fit session against these four criteria plus integration/security deep-dive, time-boxed to the Q3 selection window the buyer originally indicated.

ai_fit_signal.summary: No AIS score/status/why fields populated (all null per ground truth). Based on 14 Jan 2026 call, buyer is undergoing digital transformation (PEMS, new ERP/HRMS, eProcurement integration via API) and explicitly requested AI-driven automation (SSM/Experian vendor registration, auto-populate fields, prequalification report generation). This signals AI Curious to AI Hungry — they are digitizing end-to-end and see automation as a value driver. However, no explicit AI strategy, no AI KPIs, and no request for advanced AI features (agentic sourcing, predictive analytics) beyond the vendor-registration use case. Tier: AI Curious (leaning Hungry if digital transformation is still live).

items.detail: Buyer stated on 14 Jan 2026 call that vendor selection would start Q2/Q3 2026 and PEMS would go live Q4 2026. It is now late Jun 2026 (Q2 end), and deal has been silent for 163 days with no stage progression. If buyer's digital transformation timeline has slipped or budget has been deferred, the close date (30 Nov 2026, 157 days out) is fantasy and the deal moves to 2027+ or dies. Rep's 1 Jun note flags 'consider to drop in next 90 days', suggesting internal Zycus assessment is the deal may be lost. Immediate re-engagement required to validate timing.

If buyer re-engages, budget validation with CFO/EB is a gate before further investment of Zycus effort.

For a USD 150k–200k/year enterprise SaaS deal in a large Malaysian GLC undergoing multi-system digital transformation (PEMS + ERP + HRMS + eProcurement), C-level (CFO, CPO, GCEO office) sponsorship is mandatory. Rep's 4 Mar note mentions 'GJ working on CPO meeting after Raya' but no CPO meeting logged in 114 days since. Without executive alignment, deal cannot progress past Qualified even if operational contact re-engages.

Close date 30 Nov 2026 is 157 days out. Rep's 1 Jun note flags 'limited engagement, poor fit, consider to drop in next 90 days' — that 90-day window expires ~end Aug. One follow-up meeting logged 4 Mar 2026 (~16 weeks ago) but no recording/transcript, and no further buyer touch since. Deal has been dark for over 5 months with no stage progression.

Follow-up meeting logged 4 Mar 2026 (~16 weeks ago, 114 days ago) but no recording — cannot confirm content or engagement.

Poor fit, and consider to drop in next 90 days. 4th Mar - Met with Proc Mgr and IT team - GJ working on CPO meeting after Raya.' This is the most recent rep-initiated note; it signals the deal owner's assessment is cooling/poor-fit, not a buyer reply.

No buyer-facing deliverable executed since 14 Jan call (163 days). Promised collaterals/videos and Feb demo (committed on 14 Jan call) have no logged completion. No tasks, no emails, no further calls in 163 days.

Stage: Qualified since creation (15 Jan), no progression in 163 days. Amount $150k, close 30 Nov. Forecast: Pipeline. For a deal 157 days from close still in Qualified with zero buyer engagement in 163 days, Pipeline is generous — real category is Omitted.
Engagement pulse: COOLING per server (last activity 14 Jan, 163 days ago; rep outreach 1 Jun is rep-side note, not buyer reply). Off Track is the only defensible verdict for a deal cold 163 days.
north_star_verdict.headline: Dead deal: 163 days radio silence since 14 Jan intro call. Rep flagged 'poor fit, consider to drop' 25 days ago with 90-day drop window expiring end-August. No demo delivered, no stage progression, no champion engagement in 5+ months.

flags: VALIDATE TIMING AND COMPETITIVE SHORTLIST: Buyer stated on 14 Jan 2026 call that vendor selection would start Q2/Q3 2026 and PEMS would go live Q4 2026. It is now late Jun 2026 (Q2 end), and deal has been dark for 163 days with no progression. If buyer re-engages, confirm: (1) Is the PEMS/ERP/eProcurement roadmap still on track? (2) Is vendor selection still planned for Q3 2026? (3) Who else is being evaluated (competitive shortlist is unknown — no competitor named in 163 days)? Answers determine whether this is a live Q3/Q4 2026 close or a 2027+ long-cycle deal (or dead). If timing has slipped or budget is unfunded, re-forecast to Omitted and lower effort; if timing is live, accelerate to demo + multi-stakeholder fit session + EB intro within 30 days to catch the selection window.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal cold 163 days, rep flagged 'poor fit, consider to drop' 25 days ago. No EB, no champion, single-threaded to operational contact, no competitive intel, no stage progression in 5+ months. Close date 30 Nov (157 days out) is fantasy for a Qualified-stage deal with zero buyer engagement. Immediate re-engagement or honest disqualify required.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor identified in any source (Salesforce fields empty, no mention in calls/tasks/Next Step entries). Buyer stated on 14 Jan 2026 call they are 'checking a box of all service providers' and will initiate vendor selection Q2/Q3 2026, implying a multi-vendor shortlist, but no vendor names surfaced in 163 days. ProcureHere is the incumbent being replaced (shared license system with FGV Plantation, vendor-pay model), not a competitive alternative. With deal cold 163 days and no progression past Qualified, competitive landscape is unknown. If deal were live, Zycus wedge = enterprise eProcurement + API integration + AI automation (SSM/Experian vendor reg) + local Malaysia presence/references + ease-of-use for non-tech vendors, mapping to buyer's stated criteria. Current threat: unknown — deal has not reached evaluation.

competitors.how_we_win: Replace ProcureHere's vendor-pay friction and manual consolidation with Zycus's enterprise eProcurement + API integration with PEMS/ERP + AI-driven vendor registration (SSM/Experian auto-populate) + ease-of-use for remote/non-tech suppliers, eliminating dual-track manual work and unlocking vendor participation.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI/Merlin positioning was partially introduced on the 14 Jan 2026 call (Akshay mentioned AI agent use case for SSM/Experian vendor registration automation, Carl Kimball referenced 'agentic AI' trend in procurement), and the buyer's stated automation need (auto-populate vendor registration from due-diligence reports) is a natural AI wedge. However, no Merlin demo, no AI ROI quantification, and no AI-specific buyer appetite beyond the functional automation ask. With 163 days of silence and no follow-up, AI positioning is undeveloped. If deal re-engages, Merlin's agentic procurement (automated vendor onboarding, sourcing intelligence, spend analytics) maps directly to buyer's digital transformation and ease-of-use criteria and should be the demo centerpiece.

items.quote: Vendors may not be tech-savvy, needs to be straightforward. Suppliers in Sabah face challenges due to lack of internet connectivity.
items.source: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager
items.criterion: Ease of use for non-tech-savvy vendors (remote suppliers in Sabah with limited connectivity)

items.quote: We want e tendering system, a workable e tendering system, e bidding system, if possible.
items.source: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager

items.quote: Reporting system from reporting for the system. We need to consolidate suppliers registering in the system and also suppliers registering manually in Excel for our approved vendor list.
items.source: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager
items.criterion: Spend reporting and visibility (current manual Excel consolidation)

items.quote: Zycus API is integration friendly with our plantation estate management system. The eProcurement system will be integrated to our PEMS and new ERP.
items.source: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager
items.criterion: API integration with Plantation Estate Management System (PEMS) and future ERP/HRMS

items.quote: I'm looking for a system that can automatically do the vendor registration via pulling info from SSM, populate the eProcurement system, and provide us with the prequalification report. This would save our man hours by about half more than half a day.
items.source: Avoma intro call 14 Jan 2026, procurement manager; Akshay (Zycus product expert) confirmed feasibility: 'That's a simple AI agent use case... we need to check API availability'
items.criterion: Automated vendor registration pulling SSM due-diligence data via Experian to auto-populate fields and generate prequalification report

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Buyer's four stated criteria on 14 Jan 2026 call — ease of use for non-tech vendors, eTendering/eBidding, spend reporting, API integration with PEMS/ERP — align well with Zycus's enterprise eProcurement platform. Zycus's AI-driven vendor registration (SSM/Experian API feasibility confirmed on call by product expert Akshay) and Microsoft Teams conversational UI directly address ease-of-use and automation pain. Local Malaysia presence (Maybank, TM, DRB-Hicom, IJN references mentioned on call) and integration track record strengthen fit. However, no demo, no POC, no technical validation in 163 days; fit remains untested. Rep's 1 Jun note flags 'poor fit' but gives no specifics — this may reflect engagement/timing mismatch rather than technical misalignment, as the call showed strong criteria-solution overlap.

hard.ais_why:
• Across only 180 chars of NextStep ‚Äî a single 4 Mar 2026 entry ‚Äî the full content reads: 'Met with Proc Mgr and IT team - GJ working on CPO meeting after Raya. To share the demo video and set up the detailed demo F2F meeting in 1st week of Feb.' Zero AI mention. Product scope is eProcurement only (no AI SKUs).
• Procurement Manager and IT team have been met; CPO meeting is being arranged post-Hari Raya; demo video shared and F2F demo planned. BD_Engagement is Unengaged. With no AI products in scope and no AI dialogue captured, the deal is a focused traditional e-procurement evaluation.
• Confidence is Low because the NextStep is very thin (180 chars, one note). Score 2 reflects single-product e-procurement scope with no AI dimension whatsoever.
